reviews.comment: Dr. Afe is very knowledgeable in Issues and Concepts in Maternal and Child Health and explains complex information in a clear, relatable way. He uses strong data to discuss current global and U.S. trends and encourages students to be part of positive, impactful change.
Dr. Afe was very knowledgeable, professional, and passionate about the subject. I thoroughly enjoyed the course.

Dr. Afe conducted incredibly engaging lectures drawing from real world experiences in the field to prepare us for scenarios we will likely encounter upon graduation and entering the public health space. I gleaned so much from this course and grew as a health professional in a domain I had least expected as a result of taking this class.

Dr. Afe was an incredible professor who was understanding and compassionate, yet challenging at the same time. He was one of the most incredible professor I have had at Purdue Global and throughout my higher education. I highly recommend taking any course he is teaching.

There was a language barrier that interfered with my understanding of his assignment prompts. His use of certain words have different meanings in the English language, so interpretation of assignment prompts and discussion questions are very difficult. He also has difficulty explaining assignments in detail.
I liked her class overall, it was not bad as an introduction to psychology. Some of the course material relied too much on the textbook and was not very flexible though.
She was so rude when she said "I don't know what you were writing" publicly in class, when I asked her about my grade. Hated it.
I really enjoyed the class. She was very interactive in seminar. Course has 4 assignments that range from papers to a video recording. She gave great feedback. Be prepared to read.

Poor feedback, grades come in late. does not provide constructive criticism right away on the offense. Do not take this professor. She is not very considerate at all. Does not listen to students at all.
Professor Adell, is very unclear in her grading. She gives little to no feedback. She rarely gives 100%, nitpicky on assignments. If possible, switch to a different professor!!!!
Horrible! Does not give feedbacks. Very rude and unprofessional. Avoid her like a plague.
I would of literally not of attend this school if I knew she was a professor.

Brenda is the worst professor ever. She puts students on blast to the whole class for asking questions and she argues with students in seminar. Surprising to see the professor post her own reviews on December 5th.Course expectations and instructions were often unclear, which made the class confusing to navigate.

This professor is rude and contradictive in her lessons. She leaves early and I leave more confused. Dr. Brown was hands-down, the rudest and most unprofessional instructors I have personally had to ever deal with. She was standoffish even in the first ever “lecture”, then in following lectures she seemed like she was reading from some kind of AI generated script.

Worst Professor Ever!

Very boring and very strict

Fair in grading, yet a very demanding and blunt professor. Learned a lot, but it wasn't easy. If you want a class that really teaches you the material, then this is it. You need to be ready to put in 200% though.

reviews.comment: Very demanding during lectures. Tough grader. WOULD AVOID!

DO NOT TAKE A COURSE WITH HER. No matter how great you are on APA she will deduct mass points. We are not English students. She should not be teaching healthcare admin. I have never posted about a professor and am an A student but she was that bad. Seriously just don't do it.

One of the best professors I've encountered. His meticulous approach and high expectations pushed me to work harder and pay close attention to detail.

reviews.comment: Professor Carlin is knowledgeable, tough, fair, encouraging and most of all available to the student at a number of different times throughout the week. So there is really no excuse for not being successful in Professor Carlins' classes. I've had the pleasure of being one of his students several times through my education. Wish I could do it again.

Professor Carlin is one of the very best professors I've had. He is always helpful and provides good feedback. He is completely knowledgeable about the subject matter and will bend over backwards to help the student "get" the information being taught.

The quizzes are a bit difficult and he dose not give that much time to complete them like other professor do. However, he is understanding and allows us to go over the time as long as it is not an obsessive amount of time
Prof. Cardew was highly supportive and professional. Always answered emails promptly, and grading was fair. Work was simple, and he explained expectations very clearly in Zoom classes.
I loved professor Cardew, he was an excellent and caring teacher. Very attentive and engaging and a fair grader. I wish he taught more course would love to have him again.

Mr. Cardew was positive. He ran great online seminars, He engaged with all of his students. He didn't drone on in class. He responded promptly to any questions. Great at communication. His feedback was informative. He knows his subject very well. And he's kind. He is what a professor should be.
Prof. Cardew was approachable and professional. He graded exactly to the rubrics provided and was forthright when defining expectations.

Professor Cardew provides a great experience!  His seminars are informative and engaging, his assignments are interesting to do, and his rubrics are easy to follow.  He grades assignments directly from the rubric, and is very clear about what you're doing correctly, and what can use some improvement. I thoroughly enjoyed working with him.
Great teacher. His seminars were always very personable and he did his best to keep people engaged in the content.

She is great expectations are clear. She comes off as very strict in the beginning but is very easy to work with. She makes sure expectations are clear. I absolutely hate group projects, but she graded it fairly and ensured everyone participated.
Overall is an alright professor. She just has a tendency to be very slow when talking in lectures and often puts us in little break rooms with other students (which no one likes).

The class is not difficult, but the professor is. She takes forever to grade assignments and when she does grade them, it's not always accurate. Death by powerpoint so be ready. Professor Stringham is awesome!

The professor was very communicative and clear on expectations for assignments. Class sessions were always engaging and interesting. There is a group project in this course which was challenging but grading is fair.
Professor Claire is great, really a great professor and a kind person. Her expectations are clearly laid out and she doesn't waste your time or her own. Would gladly take more classes from her.
Professor Claire was great! She graded assignments in a timely manner and sometimes the same day.
Professor Claire is one of the best professors I have had at Purdue University.  She is clear, concise and hilarious!

I have never had a professor quite like Prof Claire. One of the most compassionate humans I have ever met! As long as you are understanding the material and putting in the effort, you will most definitely receive an "A". She is an easy grader but gives feedback to help you along. 10/10 would recommend this professor!

Does not actually teach, refers to online tutoring and writing center, never willing to help you directly. Does not follow rubric most of the time. This professor belittles her students and often times gives unhelpful feedback. She spends most of the seminar either talking about how great she is or about how much she dislikes Ai. I have never had such an issue with a professor. She constantly picks on people about APA format even if they are correct. "My students don't do the bare minimum. My students do the maximum work and that is the only work acceptable."

"People should work harder and just get their doctorate if they want more money. It is not that hard."

She drags on about the assignment requirements but never teaches the content. Huge waste of time.

Is too caught up in making her own rules for the class and trying to capture perceived evidence on students using AI to give proper feedback or advice on how to do better. Make sure you can actually attend the seminars because he will require at least two pages for the option 2 reflection. It seems a little unfair. I wouldnt recommend him as a professor. Pick someone else.

Won't help you if you need any support. She's a perfectionist for grammar but she makes rules up in her head that don't exist. I've never been docked points for grammar in my life and I'm graduating. She doesn't follow her own rubric and will assign you a 4 page paper that needs to be at least 7 pages to do what she actually wants. Pass
Good teacher who cares about her job but is a perfectionist when it comes to the work. Very harsh grader and requires active participation throughout the entire lecture in order to receive credit

Professor Lackey is a very strict professor. She has a tendency to expand outside of the rubric when it comes to grading instead of following it exactly. She is a stickler for grammar and mechanics of papers and discussion posts. Seminars are engaging, as she has prompts that must be followed for credit. Overall, I didn't enjoy having her.

Dr. Leslye grades fairly and teaches in an engaging, interactive style. Her recorded lectures are effective for retention, even for alternative assignments. A group project in her course also showcases her strong leadership style.

You get nothing out of lectures. Grading is extremely subjective and based on the way she would write. If you don't write it exactly as she would, or she doesn't think the standpoint is worthy, so many points taken off. They will probably take both student evaluation comments down for confirming that favorites are played (if it's true, it's true).

I have NEVER disliked a professor more. She is disrespectful, picks favorites, and she expects every paper to be written exactly like her. If you have an I-O degree path buckle up because you will have her as your professor at least 3 times. I dislike Purdue Global because of her. Good luck!
Great feedback, engaging and supportive.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Tough grader

reviews.comment: One of the better adjuncts in PG's EdD program (most are poor quality). The best seminars because she actually teaches, engages, and uses real-life examples (others don't). Authentic, cites sources in her insightful & instructive discussions. Friendly and helpful. Use Recite Works to help you avoid tiny APA errors that will cost you big points.

By far the most personable professor I've had. She gives good feedback and is approachable. Seminars included actual instruction, discourse on topics related to content, and tips for assignments. I recommend attending. Dr. Patel is a true educator - she knows learning is a process and helps students along the way.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This instructor was very tough about proper use of APA and I was marked down many times for small mistakes here and there. That said, you better believe I learned it well! Other than that, she was informative, interesting, and understanding of the fact that we are doctoral students with jobs and families and lives. Extensions were always given.

This professor nitpicks over every little thing. Even when I am using citations created by them, they constantly take off points for my citations being incorrect. The feedback given seems contradictory to the feedback they had given me earlier in the term. This professor seems to not know what their own expectations are so they make them up.

She is somewhat of a strict grader. She deducts points for APA format and is super strict about that. I have never received deductions for APA in any of my other courses before, but now I have. I find it strange because when I showed my assignments to other professors, they said they looked good and didn't understand why points are being taken off.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: This class was fun. I enjoyed the topics. Dr. Nelly Mulleneaux was entertaining and caring. I recommend her class.

reviews.comment: It was a great class. I am a freshman and I'm glad I took this class I hope all my classes are like this one but since I took another one at the same time I know not all professors are the same.

I never stop and give a rating like this but I feel that Dr. Mulleneaux had a great impact in my life. I'm happy I took her class. She gave us the real deal. Not like pretending but what we will truly face in the real world. No sugarcoating or anything. She told us how to face things the right way.

It is the best class I've ever taken. I've never felt so connected to a real-life experience as I did in this class. The activities gave me understanding of what the work in real life would be. Dr. Mulleneaux was there every time I had a question. She gave me clear feedback and she met with me when I needed additional help. Amazing professor.

It is the best class I've ever taken. I've never felt so connected to a real-life experience as I did in this class. The activities gave me understanding of what the work in real life would be. Dr. Mulleneaux was there every time I had a question. She gave me clear feedback and she met with me when I needed additional help. Amazing professor!

PLEASE PLEASE do not take this class. This professor is extremely manipulative and plays victim all the time. She's horrible with communicating and assigns a bunch of homework with groups that never participate so you're stuck getting a zero.

Whatever you do, don't take her. She's annoying and insufferable.

PLEASE PLEASE do not take this professor. She's extremely manipulative, annoying, and cannot speak in English clearly.

She is a really bad communicator and will never want to resolve a problem and constantly make it into something it's not. She's annoying and incompetent, never disliked a professor  iv never met in real life.
She was a great professor who I enjoyed most out of all my other classes.
I wish other professors cared the way she does. She was the greatest professor.

Professor Mulleneaux was the best professor I've ever had because she combined expertise with a genuine commitment to her students' success. She explained complex concepts in a way that was clear and practical, always connecting theory to real-world applications. She made learning engaging, encouraged open discussion.

She doesn't lecture after the first few weeks, but she's very good at getting her students involved in the class. Most of what you learn will be self taught. She offers extra credit via group projects. She's enjoyable to interact with, but make sure you turn your work in on time. Every assignment was through Cengage and you get multiple attempts.
The professor is not caring and does not seem to want to help her students.

This professor does not publish all requirements and changes requirements after you submit assignments. This professor does not grade quickly, nor answer emails unless you cc an advisor. If you get this professor, run. And that's from someone who got a good grade. It's not worth the stress.

*Does not give clear instructions when asked questions through email
*Does not give seminar credit if you do not answer all questions she asks throughout seminar (including the random color she says every 15 minutes)
*Often looks over questions in chat during seminar
*Does not allow classmates to respond to one another's questions
*Rude

Avoid if you can. This teacher is overly strict for no reason and enforces her own personal opinion into her grading. Won't respond to your emails if she finds them to be rude for some reason. Also she teaches multiple classes in the criminal justice field so if you see her name but for a different course, Yes its the same horrible professor.

Worst professor I have ever had in all my years of education. Literally makes what can be a fun class as miserable as possible. Seems to have erased the past reviews or else the rating would be even lower. Take anyone but her if you want to have a joyful schooling experience.

I just took the Applied Criminal Justice Ethics class. Kim Miller is not a nice person. She made it clear that she is in no mood for opinions or personal stories in discussions, which is funny considering this is an ethics class based on opinions. She said that if someone has a mental illness, the CJ profession is not the right choice for them.
Very rude, and strict! Has a serious grudge against AI and thinks that anyone who uses AI is a fraudster, and grades very inconsistantly.
Unorganized. Classes run long because she refuses to stay on topic.

Seminars are drawn out and half the time not spent teaching, resulting in running late every time.
Required to type redundant answers in the chat such as 'yes' to ' does everyone understand' in order to even get credit for attending the seminar. 
Seminar is at 10pm ET.
Grading is tough.
Just Awful

reviews.comment: The professor means well. She was once respected in her field and has  retained more health related knowledge than I may ever possess. That knowledge is now outdated. I've seen it in multiple courses with her, as she'll proudly trumpet a concept that's been debunked since 2004 and then recommend herbal remedies, supplements and where to buy them.

Prof. D is the Best! She treats you as an individual and is always so positive and encouraging. She understands that life happens and will always work with you. She is passionate about what she teaches. I wish I could have had her for all of my courses. After graduation, I reached out to her for guidance/support and she was there. She remembers you

Single-handedly, one of the best professors I've ever had. Her philosophy is that "learning shouldn't be stressful". And that is an important philosophy in all of her classes. She is an amazing professor, and everything that she does, and her attitude for courses are done so you can to succeed.

This class opened up my mind to understanding what healthcare is and what it could be. It touched on many topics and Dr. D had personal stories that tied into our weekly focus and helped the students understand the weekly discussion.  Her class is definitely one I would recommend to my peers and Dr. D helps make it a pleasure to take.

He will not give feedback on your papers. Awful faculty member. He copied and pasted the same comment to every student, no matter what your paper looked like. I never had such faculty I had in any of my undergrad or grad classes before. His comments don't even match the rubric provided in the assignment.
Tough grader with no feedback
Hes a really tough grader with no feedback.

Great Professor, great class. I was worried about Microbiology but this teacher was great. She says it at the beginning of class-if you do your work and show up you will get an A. Don't make my mistake and fall behind on your work.
Great Professor, great class. Took a topic I was not interested in and made me excited to show up to class and complete assignments. I am not a science person but this class was great.
I enjoyed being in her class; the seminars were very informative. Fair grader and a very understanding professor.

This class was super easy and very interesting. Dr. Christine O'Neal was a super easy grader. You were guaranteed an A if you turned something in and did your best. The seminars were pretty simple. reviews.comment: He has a terrible attitude that makes his seminars such a chore to sit through. He takes points off at will for whatever reason he feels. Not a pleasant experience.

Left the University because of him. He forgets its a learning environment and uses bad grades as a disciplinary tactic. He disapproves of student engagement and avoids clarity when it comes to grades. He is arrogant, a coward, uses AI to grade papers, and will deflect confrontation when pursued for any clarity. NO resubmission's. AVOID AT ALL COSTS

Will take off points for anything he wants, even if it is not on the rubric. Add a title page he doesn't like? Minus 5 points. Write 210 words when the range is 150-250? Minus 5 points for writing too much. Guy doesn't know what he is doing. Avoid if possible.

reviews.comment: Overall, Mr. Price is a great professor. Although, he is a harsh grader. You better have APA citing down. However, the assignments are easily manageable. His seminars are very informative. He is always available for questions, even outside of class. There are quizzes up to unit 7.
Hard grader.

reviews.comment: Awesome loved the class very good professor he teaches very well.
Best professor that any student can have very kool laid back he will make sure you understand everything in his class I would recommend him to any student he rocks.

Take him!!! He makes it so easy for you to understand!

reviews.comment: He is very specific but upfront about how he expects assignments to be. This made it very easy to get an A.

reviews.comment: He likes to joke around during the seminars but does know when to get serious. He also emails back at the drop of a dime as long as you follow the rules to email him. Like name class and what you need to ask. He doesn't do quizzes or a lot of homework, but just the right amount and actually shows examples of gold star work.

Tough grader. Feedback felt subjective, not rubric-based. Papers returned often for unclear reasons. Frustrating to guess expectations over focusing on content. Rigor is good, but consistency was lacking. Grading felt personal, hindering learning.
Very nit-picky grader ever. Stay away, don't sign up for classes for her.
Very nit-picky grader ever. Worst teacher ever
This is the most nit-picky grader ever. Prepare to redo assignments.
Extremely tough grader and nit-picky in comparison to all other previous professors. Be prepared to redo assignments multiple times.

Professor Winters was the best instructor I had in college. He makes you think, goes above and beyond to make sure you understand, and teaches lessons that make life changing differences. But understand, you will EARN your grade. Excellence comes from hard work, and his classes are worth it!

I have never seen a more over the top excessive grader. Why on earth is it a /requirement/ to get a full score on a /discussion post/ you have to post on the board on TWO SEPERATE DAYS! That alone should tell you how he grades
Worst teacher ever
My favorite professor thus far. He's hilarious, and he keeps seminars interesting. He's a fair grader, and he gives help docs!

Professor Route's in-person physics demos are fun and engaging, and the welcoming teaching environment helped to make the large lecture hall feel less imposing to a new student from a small
school like myself.

Doctor Matthew Route always stops to make sure no students have any questions, and if they do, he always does his best to answer the question, usually while writing down the math/theory
involved in the answer to help the class visually understand the answer.

This course genuinely seems to be all over the place. I genuinely dislike that Dr. Route expected you to teach yourself the material before he actually "teaches" it. The homework is never relevant
to the current unit but the next unit (hence why you have to teach yourself the content). There is not enough explanation for the math problems.

I like that Professor Route clearly enjoys physics and is passionate about it. He seemed happy to teach and it made going to lecture feel better than it would if it was required attendance and the
professor didn't want to be there either. I enjoyed the demonstrations.

The instructor was a great presenter and lecturer, he explained the content well. I liked how he gave students time to try out the example problems and time to answer each iClicker question. He
was also great at answering questions from the class.

Professor Route is very clear and straightforward on the concepts and problems of physics, and was easy to follow. He is open to students' questions and is able to relate the topics
to real life situations, making it easier to understand. I also saw him during office hours (via Zoom) and he was pretty helpful when I was stuck on a problem.

The instructor is good at breaking down problems and showing how to algebraically solve certain scenarios step-by-step. He also is good at answering any questions students might have and
incorporates answering questions throughout the lecture.
I really enjoyed the practice problems that were organized after the discussion of theories in the slides. Examples given for explanations of theories were very well explained.

He explains really well how the concepts that we learn about in the textbook can be applied to the real world and he makes sure that those examples are simple enough and really explain the concept. He does a really good job teaching the lecture and explaining any of the participation questions to make sure that students understand the information.

The instructor is good about making time to go into detailed explanations of iClicker questions during lecture. If someone asks for a better explanation, he works out the problem on paper
on the projector, explaining each step he takes to solve the problem. This method of teaching is very helpful for students.

There is no practice exams given, just a ton of formulas that he never taught you how to use. Lectures only have the basic formulas, but he expects you to just know how to manipulate them. Exams were extremely difficult and were the only reason I got a C in this course. All my other categories were 95% and above. There is no curve for this course.

Not good at teaching physics. Does not explain anything and just reads off the slides. Exam averages are 50% at best and are not curved. He never seems to know what is going on with the course in general and cannot answer questions students have. The slides are also never up to date on Brightspace, and everything I learned, I taught myself.

He is an amazing teacher with great energy who presents the material as if it was a casual conversation. Not a tough grader at all and definitely gives good and visual feedback on assignments. Was my first teacher starting at Purdue and set the tone for a great experience. I will be graduating soon and am grateful for him setting the tone!
He is a very understanding teacher and is not strict about deadlines as long as you just get all of your work in. He is super funny and loves to chat in seminars. Had a great time in this class!

This professor is clearly very passionate about his job. He's incredibly caring and helpful! Has amazing lectures and lots of interactions with students. He's always been quickly available whenever I've needed him. He's made this class so fun, it was easy for me to be successful in it. I'm so thankful I had him this term!!

Great Professor and very knowledgable. His grading is fair and consistent. Be mindful there is no sympathy for formatting errors.  Very engaging in Seminars and responsive for additional questions. Posts grades by Saturday following former week's conclusion. Would request for additional courses if available.
Great professor, I'm glad I got him for one of my first classes in college.

Gives a lot of additional info and resources in discussion boards and emails. As long as you put in the effort and double check your spelling and grammar he is in an easy grader. Keeps seminars fun as long as you're paying attention.

His seminars are engaging, and the time seems to move too quickly each week! He is always providing answers to questions, even if they are not related to the actual topic in the weekly seminar. He is genuinely one of the best professors I've ever had!
He's absolutely awesome!
Professor Tarhan was my favorite undergraduate Professor so far. His seminars were interesting and had a debate-like feel. I would be more than happy to take another class of his.

reviews.comment: One of the best Professors at PGU. Seminars are a blast and there is so much information to go over. Very critical with grading assignments so check and check again your grammar and correct spelling. If I could score a 10 I would. Very informative and is easy to communicate with.
I actually had Karen Savarese for two classes, PS124 and PS330 (neither is on the dropdown menu), and I wish she taught everything!
Professed Savrese is amazing I loved taking PS220 with her

reviews.comment: She is an amazing professor!

I'd definitely love to work with her again!
Great teacher.

Dr. Wheeler has created a practical, real-life applicable business strategy course at Purdue Global. He clearly stated all assignment requirements and expectations and was consistently encouraging.

This professor is amazing. He makes the assignments easy to understand and offers assistance for anything you need help on. If I had any more classes to take I would definitely try to take one of his classes again. Great teacher.
Very good at what he does. Easy to learn and if you follow the process you will do good in his class. Very understanding and willing to work with you the best he can. Loved his class.

Professor Wheeler was:

Very informative. When you follow his instructions and deadlines you will be guaranteed an A.
This professor will do everything in his power to make the class load as light as possible. He also provides you with the necessary materials to achieve success.

Professor Wheeler passionately teaches writing process and tools and clearly communicates expectations and outcomes. He rewards participation and solicits feedback from students. Ten out of ten, highly recommend.

Professor Tedd Wheeler Reflects exceptional professionalism & care for the investment of your academic career. He is very helpful & thoughtful. He teaches excellent & rigorously. I would recommend a professor like him to all students at Purdue University Global.

I wish I could take her class again! Excellent teacher!!
One of the warmest and brightest auras a teacher has had. Simply logging in and seeing her smiling puts me in such a better mood.

I wish not ever to have him again!
